wiki_catalogs.name: Project Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the Account Financial Reports module overview. Explain its purpose as an Odoo ERP extension that provides enterprise-grade financial reporting capabilities beyond standard Odoo reports. Document the module's architecture, key features including General Ledger, Trial Balance, Open Items, Aged Partner Balance, VAT Report, and Journal Ledger. Describe the target audience (accountants, finance professionals, ERP administrators) and primary use cases. Include the module's relationship to Odoo's core accounting system and its role in financial compliance and reporting workflows. Provide practical examples of how organizations benefit from enhanced financial reporting capabilities.

wiki_catalogs.name: General Ledger Report
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the General Ledger Report specification. Detail the report's transaction-level detail functionality, including how it processes account move lines with cumulative balances and partner grouping options. Document the data fields including account codes, dates, descriptions, debit/credit amounts, and running balances. Explain the filtering capabilities for date ranges, accounts, partners, and journals. Cover the multi-currency support and exchange rate handling. Detail the output formats including PDF, HTML, and XLSX exports. Document the wizard configuration options for report parameters, grouping settings, and display preferences. Include technical specifications for data aggregation, sorting algorithms, and performance optimizations. Provide examples of typical use cases and common configurations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Abstract Report Framework
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the abstract report framework that serves as the foundation for all financial reports in the module. Explain the AbstractModel inheritance pattern and how it provides common functionality across all report types. Document the core methods including _get_move_lines_domain_not_reconciled, _get_new_move_lines_domain, and _recalculate_move_lines that handle data filtering and reconciliation calculations. Detail the COMMON_ML_FIELDS constant and its role in standardizing move line field access. Explain the _get_accounts_data and _get_journals_data methods for retrieving master data. Document the Excel export functionality in abstract_report_xlsx.py and how it extends the base report capabilities. Include practical examples of how individual report implementations inherit from this framework and override specific methods.

wiki_catalogs.name: Getting Started
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive getting started documentation for new users of the Account Financial Reports module. Explain what the module provides beyond standard Odoo financial reports, including advanced reporting capabilities like General Ledger, Trial Balance, Open Items, Aged Partner Balance, VAT Report, and Journal Ledger. Document prerequisites including Odoo version requirements, installed modules, and basic accounting knowledge needed. Provide step-by-step installation instructions, initial configuration steps, and basic navigation guidance. Include screenshots of the main menu locations and initial setup screens. Explain how to access reports from the Invoicing menu and introduce the wizard-based configuration interface that is common across all report types.

wiki_catalogs.name: Aging Report Configuration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for configuring aging report parameters in the Account Financial Reports module. Detail the aging report configuration model including how to define custom aging periods, configure interval lengths, and set up aging criteria. Explain the user interface for managing aging configurations through Odoo's view system. Document the relationship between aging configurations and report generation, including how different aging setups affect report output. Include step-by-step instructions for creating new aging configurations, modifying existing ones, and testing configuration changes. Provide examples of common aging period setups for different business requirements such as 30/60/90 day aging, monthly intervals, or custom period lengths. Address validation rules, constraint handling, and best practices for aging configuration management.

wiki_catalogs.name: Report Generation API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API documentation for the report generation interfaces in the Account Financial Reports module. Document the abstract report base class methods including _get_move_lines_domain_not_reconciled(), _get_new_move_lines_domain(), _recalculate_move_lines(), _get_accounts_data(), _get_journals_data(), and _get_ml_fields(). Detail the method signatures, parameter specifications, return value descriptions, and usage examples for each public interface. Include the report processing pipeline, data aggregation functions, and template integration methods. Provide code examples demonstrating how to extend the abstract report class for custom report implementations. Document error handling strategies, performance considerations, and integration patterns with Odoo's reporting framework.

wiki_catalogs.name: Getting Started
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive getting started documentation for the Account Financial Reports module. Include detailed installation instructions for Odoo environments, prerequisite requirements (Odoo version compatibility, required core modules), dependency management, and initial setup procedures. Document the module activation process, basic configuration steps, and first-time user setup. Provide step-by-step guidance for accessing the financial reporting interface, understanding the main menu structure, and generating your first report. Include troubleshooting tips for common installation issues and verification steps to ensure proper module installation.

wiki_catalogs.name: Trial Balance Report
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the Trial Balance Report specification. Explain the report's account hierarchy handling and how it aggregates account balances across selected periods. Document the unaffected earnings integration and how retained earnings are handled in the calculation. Detail the data fields including account codes, account names, debit balances, credit balances, and ending balances. Explain the grouping options for account hierarchies and how parent-child relationships are displayed. Cover the filtering capabilities for date ranges, account groups, and company contexts. Document the output formats and formatting options for hierarchical display. Include technical specifications for the balance calculation algorithms, currency handling, and performance considerations. Provide examples of typical trial balance scenarios and configuration options.

wiki_catalogs.name: Data Models and Business Logic
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the data models and business logic that power the financial reporting functionality. Document the account model extensions that add reporting capabilities, the account_move_line modifications for enhanced filtering and aggregation, and the account_group model for hierarchical account organization. Explain the ir_actions_report model integration for report actions and the res_config_settings model for module configuration. Detail the data relationships, field definitions, and business rules that govern financial report generation. Include examples of how these models interact during report processing, data validation rules, and performance optimization strategies. Document the custom methods added to existing Odoo models and their impact on report functionality.

wiki_catalogs.name: Report Generation Workflow
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation explaining the universal report generation workflow used across all financial reports in the module. Document the three-phase process: 1) Wizard configuration where users set report parameters and filters, 2) Data processing where the system validates inputs and prepares report data, and 3) Report generation where the final output is produced in selected format. Explain how to navigate between different report types, understand common configuration options like date ranges, companies, and currencies, and how to preview reports before final generation. Include troubleshooting tips for common configuration errors and best practices for report setup.

wiki_catalogs.name: Global Settings and Preferences
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for global configuration settings and preferences in the Account Financial Reports module. Document the Odoo settings model that controls module-wide behavior, including default report options, display preferences, and system-wide parameters. Explain how to access and modify settings through Odoo's configuration interface. Detail the relationship between module settings and individual report configurations. Include guidance on setting default values for report parameters, configuring multi-company behavior, and managing user-specific preferences. Document security implications of different settings and best practices for configuration management. Provide examples of common configuration scenarios such as setting default date ranges, configuring currency display options, and establishing company-specific report defaults. Address settings validation, backup procedures, and migration considerations when upgrading the module.

wiki_catalogs.name: Wizard Configuration API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed API documentation for the wizard configuration interfaces used in report setup and parameter collection. Document the abstract wizard base class methods, parameter validation functions, and report preparation utilities. Detail the wizard field definitions, validation logic, and domain construction methods. Include method signatures for wizard actions, data transformation functions, and report generation triggers. Provide usage examples showing how to configure report parameters through wizard interfaces. Document the integration between wizard inputs and report execution, error handling for invalid configurations, and best practices for extending wizard functionality.

wiki_catalogs.name: User Guide
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed user guide documentation covering the complete workflow for generating financial reports. Document the wizard-based configuration interface used across all report types, explaining how to navigate between different report wizards, configure report parameters, apply filters, and customize report settings. Include step-by-step instructions for each report type with screenshots and practical examples. Cover advanced features like multi-currency handling, date range selection, partner filtering, account grouping options, and output format preferences. Explain the relationship between wizard configurations and final report generation, including preview functionality and report customization options.

wiki_catalogs.name: Open Items Report
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the Open Items Report specification. Detail the report's outstanding item tracking functionality and how it identifies unpaid invoices and bills. Document the data fields including partner information, invoice numbers, due dates, original amounts, paid amounts, and outstanding balances. Explain the aging analysis capabilities and how overdue items are categorized. Cover the filtering options for partners, date ranges, invoice types, and aging periods. Detail the output formats and how the report displays detailed transaction histories for each open item. Document the wizard configuration for selecting partners, applying filters, and setting aging criteria. Include technical specifications for the outstanding balance calculations, aging algorithms, and performance optimizations for large datasets. Provide examples of typical open items analysis scenarios.

wiki_catalogs.name: Security and Access Control
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the security model and access control mechanisms in the Account Financial Reports module. Document the security.xml configuration that defines report access permissions and the ir.model.access.csv file that manages object-level permissions. Explain the user roles and groups that can access different report types, the permission hierarchy, and how security is enforced at both the menu and report action levels. Detail the data visibility rules that ensure users only see relevant financial information based on their company and access rights. Include examples of configuring custom security policies, troubleshooting access issues, and best practices for maintaining secure financial reporting environments.

wiki_catalogs.name: General Ledger Report
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the General Ledger report configuration and usage. Explain how to configure transaction-level detail reporting, including filtering by account groups, partners, and date ranges. Document the cumulative balances feature and how they are calculated and displayed. Cover partner grouping options and foreign currency support including exchange rate handling. Explain the difference between showing all accounts versus only those with activity. Include practical examples of common General Ledger scenarios like monthly reconciliations, audit trails, and detailed transaction tracking. Document output format options including PDF, HTML, and XLSX with their respective advantages.

wiki_catalogs.name: Frontend Customization
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for customizing the frontend appearance and behavior of financial reports in the Account Financial Reports module. Detail the CSS styling system including report-specific stylesheets and HTML rendering styles. Explain the JavaScript enhancement system covering report actions, dynamic content loading, and interactive features. Document the XML template system for report layout customization and content modification. Include guidance on overriding default styles, adding custom CSS classes, and implementing responsive design considerations. Provide examples of common frontend customizations such as adding logos, custom headers/footers, color schemes, and layout modifications. Document the asset pipeline integration and how to properly package custom frontend resources. Address browser compatibility, performance optimization, and accessibility considerations for report customization. Include troubleshooting guides for common frontend customization issues and best practices for maintaining customizations during module updates.

wiki_catalogs.name: Data Model Interfaces
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API documentation for the data model interfaces used by the financial reporting module. Document the account model extensions, move line data structures, and report configuration models. Detail the field definitions, relationship mappings, and data validation rules for each model. Include method signatures for data access functions, query optimization utilities, and data transformation methods. Provide examples of model inheritance patterns, custom field additions, and data filtering operations. Document the integration with Odoo's ORM, performance considerations for large datasets, and best practices for extending model functionality.

wiki_catalogs.name: Technical Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create architectural documentation for the Account Financial Reports module's technical design. Describe the high-level architecture including the abstract report framework, data processing pipeline, and template rendering system. Document the component interactions between models, wizards, reports, and templates. Explain the inheritance hierarchy starting from abstract base classes and how individual report implementations extend core functionality. Detail the data flow from wizard configuration through report preparation to final output generation. Include technical decisions around multi-currency support, date range handling, and report caching mechanisms. Address the security model, access controls, and permission management.

wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the Aged Partner Balance Report specification. Explain the report's receivables aging functionality and how it categorizes outstanding amounts into customizable aging periods. Document the age configuration system and how users can define custom aging intervals. Detail the data fields including partner information, aging buckets, amounts per period, total outstanding, and aging percentages. Explain the dynamic configuration options for aging periods and how they affect the report calculations. Cover the filtering capabilities for partners, date ranges, and company contexts. Document the output formats and how aging buckets are displayed and calculated. Detail the age report configuration model and how aging settings are stored and managed. Provide examples of typical aging analysis scenarios and configuration setups.

wiki_catalogs.name: Report Generation Pipeline
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the complete report generation pipeline from user input to final output. Explain the data flow from wizard configuration through report preparation to template rendering and output generation. Document the interaction between abstract_wizard.py and individual report implementations, showing how configuration options are processed and validated. wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the Trial Balance report configuration and functionality. Include practical examples of using the Trial Balance for month-end closing procedures and financial statement preparation. wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API reference documentation for the Account Financial Reports module's programmatic interfaces. Detail the wizard configuration APIs including parameter validation, report preparation methods, and domain construction utilities. wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive deployment and maintenance documentation for the Account Financial Reports module. wiki_overview.content:
Based on my comprehensive analysis of the Odoo financial reporting project, I can now provide a detailed overview:

<blog>

# Account Financial Reports - Odoo Module Analysis

## Project Introduction

### Purpose Statement
The Account Financial Reports module is a comprehensive Odoo extension that provides advanced financial reporting capabilities beyond the standard Odoo financial statements. It offers enterprise-grade reporting features for accounting professionals and financial analysts.

### Core Goals and Objectives
- **Enhanced Financial Reporting**: Deliver sophisticated financial reports including General Ledger, Trial Balance, Open Items, Aged Partner Balance, VAT Report, and Journal Ledger
- **Multi-Currency Support**: Handle foreign currency configurations and display balances accurately across different currencies
- **Flexible Filtering**: Provide extensive filtering options for accounts, partners, journals, and analytical dimensions
- **Customizable Presentation**: Support various grouping options (partners, taxes) and presentation formats

### Target Audience
- Financial controllers and accountants
- Business analysts requiring detailed financial insights
- Companies needing compliance reporting
- Organizations operating in multiple currencies

## Technical Architecture

### Component Breakdown

```mermaid
graph TD
    A[Odoo Backend] --> B[Report Engine]
    B --> C[Abstract Report Base]
    C --> D[Individual Report Classes]
    D --> E[General Ledger]
    D --> F[Trial Balance]
    D --> G[Open Items]
    D --> H[Aged Partner Balance]
    D --> I[VAT Report]
    D --> J[Journal Ledger]
    
    K[Wizard Layer] --> L[Report Configuration]
    L --> M[Filter Selection]
    L --> N[Output Format]
    
    O[Template Layer] --> P[XML Templates]
    P --> Q[HTML Layouts]
    P --> R[PDF Generation]
    
    S[Data Models] --> T[Account Models]
    S --> U[Configuration Models]
    S --> V[Report Data Structures]
```

### Design Patterns

The module follows several key design patterns:

1. **Template Method Pattern**: Abstract report classes define common workflows while individual reports implement specific behaviors
2. **Strategy Pattern**: Different grouping strategies (partners, taxes, none) are handled through configurable parameters
3. **Factory Pattern**: Report wizards dynamically create appropriate report instances based on user selections
4. **Observer Pattern**: Event-driven updates for domain changes and validation

### System Relationships

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant User
    participant Wizard
    participant ReportEngine
    participant DataLayer
    participant TemplateEngine
    
    User->>Wizard: Configure Report Parameters
    Wizard->>ReportEngine: Prepare Report Data
    ReportEngine->>DataLayer: Query Financial Data
    DataLayer-->>ReportEngine: Processed Financial Data
    ReportEngine->>TemplateEngine: Render Report
    TemplateEngine-->>User: Generated Report
```

## Key Implementation

### Main Entry Points
- **Manifest File**: `/__manifest__.py` - Module definition and dependencies
- **Report Registry**: `/report/__init__.py` - Central report registration
- **Wizard Registry**: `/wizard/__init__.py` - Wizard module initialization

### Core Modules

#### Abstract Report Framework
- **Base Class**: `/report/abstract_report.py` - Common report functionality and data processing
- **Excel Export**: `/report/abstract_report_xlsx.py` - XLSX export capabilities

#### Individual Report Implementations
- **General Ledger**: `/report/general_ledger.py` - Comprehensive transaction detail reporting
- **Trial Balance**: `/report/trial_balance.py` - Account-wise balance summarization
- **Open Items**: `/report/open_items.py` - Outstanding receivable/payable tracking
- **Aged Partner Balance**: `/report/aged_partner_balance.py` - Receivables aging analysis
- **VAT Report**: `/report/vat_report.py` - Value Added Tax reporting
- **Journal Ledger**: `/report/journal_ledger.py` - Journal-specific transaction tracking

### Configuration Approach
- **Security Rules**: `/security/security.xml` - Access control and data visibility
- **Access Control**: `/security/ir.model.access.csv` - Permission management
- **Model Definitions**: `/models/` - Data model specifications and relationships

### External Dependencies
- **Odoo Core**: Depends on `account`, `date_range`, and `report_xlsx` modules
- **Build System**: `/pyproject.toml` - Modern Python packaging with Whool

### Integration Points
- **Odoo ORM**: Direct integration with Odoo's database abstraction layer
- **Report Engine**: Utilizes Odoo's native reporting framework
- **Asset Pipeline**: Frontend JavaScript and CSS integration for enhanced UX

### Component Relationships

```mermaid
classDiagram
    class AbstractReport {
        +COMMON_ML_FIELDS
        +_get_move_lines_domain_not_reconciled()
        +_get_new_move_lines_domain()
        +_recalculate_move_lines()
        +_get_accounts_data()
        +_get_journals_data()
        +_get_ml_fields()
    }
    
    class GeneralLedgerReport {
        +_get_analytic_data()
        +_get_taxes_data()
        +_get_initial_balances_bs_ml_domain()
        +_get_period_ml_data()
        +_create_general_ledger()
    }
    
    class GeneralLedgerReportWizard {
        +date_from
        +date_to
        +account_ids
        +partner_ids
        +centralize
        +foreign_currency
        +_prepare_report_general_ledger()
    }
    
    AbstractReport <|-- GeneralLedgerReport
    AbstractReport <|-- AbstractReportXlsx
    GeneralLedgerReportWizard --> GeneralLedgerReport : generates
```

## Key Features

### Functionality Overview

#### Comprehensive Financial Reporting Suite
- **General Ledger**: Detailed transaction-level reporting with cumulative balances
- **Trial Balance**: Account-wise balance summarization for period-end closing
- **Open Items**: Outstanding receivable and payable tracking with aging analysis
- **Aged Partner Balance**: Receivables aging by predefined intervals
- **VAT Report**: Value Added Tax calculations and reporting
- **Journal Ledger**: Journal-specific transaction tracking and analysis

#### Advanced Filtering and Grouping
- Multi-dimensional filtering by accounts, partners, journals, and analytical centers
- Flexible grouping options (partners, taxes, none) for detailed analysis
- Custom date range and fiscal year support
- Foreign currency handling with automatic conversion

### Implementation Highlights

#### Data Processing Architecture
- **Domain-Based Filtering**: `/report/general_ledger.py` - Sophisticated query construction for optimal performance
- **Accrual vs Cash Basis**: `/report/abstract_report.py` - Support for different accounting bases
- **Centralization Engine**: `/report/general_ledger.py` - Automatic monthly consolidation for large datasets

#### User Interface Components
- **Wizard Interfaces**: `/wizard/` - Intuitive configuration dialogs with real-time validation
- **Template System**: `/report/templates/` - Extensible HTML/PDF template architecture
- **Frontend Enhancement**: `/static/src/js/` - Interactive JavaScript enhancements

### Feature Architecture

```mermaid
stateDiagram-v2
    [*] --> Configuration
    Configuration --> Validation
    Validation --> DataProcessing
    DataProcessing --> ReportGeneration
    ReportGeneration --> OutputSelection
    OutputSelection --> [*]
    
    Configuration --> Error: Invalid Input
    Error --> Configuration
    
    DataProcessing --> Error: No Data Found
    Error --> Configuration
```

The module demonstrates excellent separation of concerns with clear boundaries between data processing, presentation, and user interaction layers. The architecture supports extensibility while maintaining performance through optimized database queries and efficient data structures.

**Sources:**
- [__manifest__.py](/__manifest__.py)
- [abstract_report.py](/report/abstract_report.py)
- [general_ledger.py](/report/general_ledger.py)
- [general_ledger_wizard.py](/wizard/general_ledger_wizard.py)
- [general_ledger.xml](/report/templates/general_ledger.xml)
- [test_general_ledger.py](/tests/test_general_ledger.py)
- [security.xml](/security/security.xml)

</blog>

wiki_repo.name: account_financial_report

├── i18n/
